zh.lifeSummary: 當Faelis ferch Llywellyn of Wales於大約1025年出生在威爾士蒙姆爾蒙姆斯時，她的父親Llywelyn ap Llywellyn27歲，母親Gwenllian ferch John27歲。她於大約1050年與Rhiwallon ap Cynfyn在英國威爾斯波斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1081年在英國威爾斯波斯布雷孔享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Faelis ferch Llywellyn of Wales于大约1025年出生在威尔士蒙姆爾蒙姆斯时，她的父亲Llywelyn ap Llywellyn27岁，母亲Gwenllian ferch John27岁。她于大约1050年与Rhiwallon ap Cynfyn在英国威尔斯波斯结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1081年在英国威尔斯波斯布雷孔享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: ferch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Ferg  . Compare   Verch  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förtsch (see Foertsch ).
Probably also an Americanized form of German Förch (see Foerch ).

variants.name: Ferg
meanings: German: occupational name for a ferryman, Middle High German verje, verge.

nameMeanings.name: Llywellyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Llywelyn, derived from British Celtic Lugubelinos, itself from the divine name Lugus + an uncertain second element often used alone as a name. Through association with llew ‘lion’, it was altered to Llewelyn. This was the name of two princes of north Wales who held the Normans at bay for many years in the 13th century. Etymologically speaking, the form with a fourth -l- is an error, not found as an original Welsh name, but this is now much the most common spelling in the US.

variants.name: Lewelling
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Llewellyn  .

variants.name: Lewellen
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Llewellyn  .

variants.name: Lewallen
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Llewellyn  .

variants.name: Louallen
meanings: Altered form of Welsh Llewelyn (see Llewellyn ).

variants.name: Luellen
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Llewellyn  .

variants.name: Welling
meanings: English: habitational name from Welwyn (Hertfordshire) or Welling in Bexley (Kent), with a suspicion of an alternative unidentified source. The Hertfordshire placename means ‘at the willow trees’, from Old English wel(i)gum, dative plural form of welig ‘willow’. The Kent placename may derive from Old English wella ‘well, spring, stream’ + the placename forming suffix -ing. In some cases this name may represent a development of Llewellyn , particularly near the Welsh border.
German: habitational name from any of various places in the Rhineland called Welling or Wellingen.

variants.name: Luallen
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Llewellyn  .

variants.name: Lewellyn
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Llewellyn  .

variants.name: Llewelyn
meanings: Welsh: variant of   Llewellyn  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Wales
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English:

habitational name from Wales near Sheffield (South Yorkshire). The placename means ‘The Welshmen’ and denoted a settlement of Brittonic speakers living on the boundary between the Anglo-Saxon kingdoms of Northumbria and Mercia (from Old English wēalas, walas, plural of walh, wealh, originally meaning ‘Roman citizen’ and referring to the native British population, but in Old English this term later came to mean ‘serf, unfree person, foreigner’ or ‘Welshman’). See also Wallis .
habitational name from Wales, the country named from Old English wēalas ‘foreigners’ (especially those speaking Celtic or Romance languages). This meaning of the surname is quite rare.

variants.name: Wailes
meanings: English: variant of   Wales  .

variants.name: Wallis
meanings: Scottish and English: from Anglo-Norman French Waleis, Walais ‘Welshman’, also sometimes ‘Breton’ (from Old English wēalas, walas, plural of walh, wealh, originally meaning a ‘Roman citizen’ and referring to the native British population, but in Old English this term later came to mean ‘serf, unfree person, foreigner’ or ‘Welshman’). In western and central England the medieval reference is clearly to Welshmen. In Norfolk and Lincolnshire, the reference was probably to Bretons, many of whom settled in the eastern counties after the Conquest. Compare Welsh , Walsh . The idea that the Scottish surname refers to the Welsh-speaking Britons of Strathclyde is erroneous.

nameMeanings.name: Faelis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Anglo-Norman French faleise, faloise ‘cliff’ (a word of ancient Germanic origin), for someone who lived on or by a cliff. Or from Falaise in Calvados, France, which was the birthplace of William the Conqueror.
English: variant of   Fall   2.
Scottish and northern Irish: shortened form of   McFalls   (see also   McPhail  ).

variants.name: Falls
meanings: Anglo-Norman: An Anglo-Norman form of Philip.

variants.name: Fallas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: transferred use of the surname, adopted in honour of George Mifflin Dallas , Vice-President 1845–49 , after whom the city in Texas is named. The surname is of Scottish origin, derived from the village of Dallas in Morayshire, named in Gaelic as Dalfhas ‘meadow stance’, i.e. a meadow traditionally used as a night's resting place by cattle drovers.

variants.name: Follis
meanings: Scottish and Irish: variant of Fallis or a shortened form of McFalls , both of which are derived from McPhail , from Scottish Gaelic Mac Phàil or Irish Mac Phóil, ‘son of Paul’.

variants.name: Fall
meanings: Anglo-Norman: An Anglo-Norman form of Philip.

en.lifeSummary: When Faelis ferch Llywellyn of Wales was born about 1025, in Monmouth, Monmouthshire, Wales, her father, Llywelyn ap Llywellyn, was 27 and her mother, Gwenllian ferch John, was 27. She married Rhiwallon ap Cynfyn about 1050, in Powys, Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1081, in Brecon, Powys, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 57.

fr.Brecon, Powys, Wales: Brecon, Powys, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Faelis ferch Llywellyn of Wales est née environ 1025, à Monmouth, Monmouthshire, Galles, son père, Llywelyn ap Llywellyn, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Gwenllian ferch John, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Rhiwallon ap Cynfyn environ 1050, à Powys, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1081, à Brecon, Powys, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Faelis ferch Llywellyn of Wales um 1025 in Monmouth, Monmouthshire, Wales geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Llywelyn ap Llywellyn 27 und ihre Mutter Gwenllian ferch John 27. Sie hat um 1050 in Powys, Wales, United Kingdom Rhiwallon ap Cynfyn geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1081 im Alter von 57 Jahren in Brecon, Powys, Wales, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Faelis ferch Llywellyn of Wales è nata il circa 1025 nel Monmouth, Monmouthshire, Galles, sua padre, Llywelyn ap Llywellyn, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Gwenllian ferch John, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Rhiwallon ap Cynfyn circa 1050, nel Powys, Galles, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1081, nel Brecon, Powys, Galles, Regno Unito, all'età di 57 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Faelis ferch Llywellyn of Walesが約1025年にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアモンモウスで生まれた時，父Llywelyn ap Llywellynは27歳，母Gwenllian ferch Johnは27歳であった。約1050年にイギリスウェールズポウイスでRhiwallon ap Cynfynと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1081年にイギリスウェールズポウイスブレコンで57歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Faelis ferch Llywellyn of Wales(이)가 대략 1025년 웨일스 몬모우스실르 몬모우스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Llywelyn ap Llywellyn(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Gwenllian ferch John(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 대략 1050년 영국 웨일스 파우이스에서 Rhiwallon ap Cynfyn(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1081년 영국 웨일스 파우이스 브레콘에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Faelis ferch Llywellyn of Wales nasceu aproximadamente 1025, em Monmouth, Monmouthshire, Gales, seu pai, Llywelyn ap Llywellyn, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Gwenllian ferch John, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Rhiwallon ap Cynfyn aproximadamente 1050, em Powys, Gales, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Faelis ferch Llywellyn of Wales родилась около 1025 г. в местности монмоут, монмоутшир, Уэльс, её отцу по имени Llywelyn ap Llywellyn было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Gwenllian ferch John, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Rhiwallon ap Cynfyn около 1050 г. в местности поуис, Уэльс, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1081 г. в местности брекон, поуис, Уэльс, Великобритания в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Faelis ferch Llywellyn of Wales nació aproximadamente 1025, en Monmouth, Monmouthshire, Gales, su papá, Llywelyn ap Llywellyn, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Gwenllian ferch John, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Rhiwallon ap Cynfyn aproximadamente 1050, en Powys, Gales, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1081, en Brecon, Powys, Gales, Reino Unido, a la edad de 57 años.
